isolation_confirmation.statement: Only the protocol prompt and sanitized questions were used. No keys, feedback, scores, prior attempts, other agents' files, or empirical candidate results were inspected.

mlp.assumptions: depth counts constant-width hidden affine layers; each affine and scalar output has bias; each enabled LayerNorm has trainable scale and bias; residual paths add no parameters.

transformer_lm.formula: P=V*d + C*d + L*(4*d^2 + 2*d*d_ff + 9*d + d_ff) + d*V + V + 2*d
transformer_lm.expansion: token embedding Vd + learned position embedding Cd + per layer [attention 4d^2+4d, FFN 2d*d_ff+d_ff+d, two LayerNorms 4d] + untied LM head dV+V + final LayerNorm 2d.
transformer_lm.assumptions: standard biased projections, learned positions, two norms per block, final norm, untied output head; head count changes partitioning but not parameter count at fixed d.

bigram_lm.score_expression: capacity_fit + optimizer_suitability + learning_rate_fit + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + scale_lr_interaction + depth_budget_interaction + step_budget_centered

capacity_fit.definition: Piecewise by exact transformer parameter count P.

architecture_fit.definition: d_model lookup + layers lookup + d_ff lookup.

step_budget_centered.definition: 0.2*log2(steps/geometric_mean_steps_in_question); it is 0 because steps are fixed across every visible choice.

interaction_terms.condition: SGD lr=0.01 with momentum and P<100k

bigram_lm.rationale: A first-order source needs enough capacity but not a very large transformer; with only 80 updates, optimizer/LR and scale-trainability interactions dominate nominal depth.

multivariate_regression.score_expression: capacity_fit + optimizer_suitability + learning_rate_fit + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + scale_lr_interaction + depth_budget_interaction + step_budget_centered

capacity_fit.definition: Piecewise by exact MLP parameter count P.

architecture_fit.definition: depth lookup + 0.25 for residual when depth>=3 + normalization term + 0.4*(GELU+SiLU fraction)+0.1*(leaky-ReLU fraction).

step_budget_centered.definition: 0.2*log2(steps/geometric_mean_steps_in_question); it is 0 because all choices have 160 steps.

multivariate_regression.rationale: The noiseless 4-D target contains smooth periodic terms and interactions, favoring moderate capacity, smooth activations, and optimizers that can use 160 steps efficiently.

univariate_regression.score_expression: capacity_fit + optimizer_suitability + learning_rate_fit + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + scale_lr_interaction + depth_budget_interaction + step_budget_centered

capacity_fit.definition: Piecewise by exact MLP parameter count P.

architecture_fit.definition: depth lookup + 0.15 residual bonus for depth>=3 + LN term + 0.5*(GELU+SiLU fraction)+0.1*(leaky-ReLU fraction).

step_budget_centered.definition: 0.2*log2(steps/geometric_mean_steps_in_question); it is 0 because all choices have 64 steps.

interaction_terms.condition: SGD lr=0.01 with momentum and P<15k

univariate_regression.rationale: One smooth one-cycle target needs modest basis capacity, but the 64-step budget strongly favors useful LR/optimizer pairs and penalizes very large networks.

anomalies_found: All repeated candidate IDs received identical initial scores; no candidate-specific exception was introduced.
Steps and batch size are invariant within every visible question, so the centered step contribution is exactly zero and cannot spuriously decide a question.
Bigram transformer complexity appeared in parameter capacity, architecture, and depth-budget terms; the largest-capacity benefits were therefore reduced rather than adding another independent risk.

In multivariate regression, exact counts exposed a discontinuity: a 256-wide one-layer MLP has only about two thousand parameters but many usable basis units, so the 500-2500 band and fast-adaptive interaction needed revision.
For univariate regression, positive capacity credit above 60k parameters was implausible under 64 steps and a simple one-cycle target.
No single optimizer term exceeded the combined feasible range of capacity plus architecture; optimizer and LR remain separate so nominal LR is not rewarded without optimizer suitability.
proposed_changes: Strengthen large-model and extreme-adaptive-LR risks in bigram_lm.
Credit wide-shallow basis capacity, smooth activation match, and fast adaptive trainability in multivariate_regression while strengthening slow-large undertraining risk.
Remove positive overcapacity credit and strengthen slow-large undertraining risk in univariate_regression.
consistency_audit.audit_outcome: Shared family formulas remained globally consistent; all proposed changes were applied uniformly and every question was re-scored.

formula_revisions.reason: Reduce the moderate-large transformer benefit after the audit found complexity represented in both parameter capacity and depth-budget terms.
Make the 80-step undertraining risk explicit for the largest transformers.
The initial LR lookup penalized 0.01, but did not express its added instability when paired with adaptive transformer optimization.
Exact counts showed that wide one-hidden-layer networks have few parameters despite many basis units; the initial band under-credited their usable capacity.
The visible target is explicitly tanh/sine/cosine based, so smooth activation alignment deserved a slightly larger but still bounded term.

formula_revisions.feature: Adam/AdamW lr=0.003 bonus lower P threshold
formula_revisions.reason: The old threshold excluded a 256-wide shallow network even though it can use the fast optimizer within 160 steps.
The audit found the initial capacity term partially offset a clear short-budget undertraining interaction for very large MLPs.
For only 64 updates and a one-cycle scalar target, very large MLPs should not receive a positive capacity benefit.
A larger slow-learning adaptive model is especially unlikely to exploit its capacity in 64 steps.

final_reflection.main_drivers: Optimizer-specific non-monotonic LR fit and capacity-versus-step-budget interactions drove most decisions; exact parameter count, depth, smooth activations, residual/LN support, and weight decay broke close comparisons.
final_reflection.changes: 1 of 60 letters changed after the label-free revision.
final_reflection.uncertainties: The rule cannot observe initialization, gradient scale, or realized optimization trajectories. Parameter counting also assumes conventional biased MLP/transformer modules and an untied LM head; alternative implementation details would shift counts slightly but not the shared ranking logic.
final_reflection.isolation: No empirical results or labels informed either pass.
